craft_guidance: 1. IDENTIFY OUTPUT CONTEXT: HTML body, attribute (quoted/unquoted), JS string, JS template literal, URL, CSS, comment/CDATA.
2. BREAK THE CONTEXT: HTML=<tag>, attribute=' or " escape, JS=' or "+;, template=${}, URL=javascript: or data:.
3. CHECK ENCODING: Test < > ' " & / ( ) individually. If < > blocked but ' is not, use attribute context (onfocus=alert(1) autofocus).
4. EVENT HANDLERS: <script> blocked? Use onerror, onload, onfocus+autofocus, ontoggle, onmouseover. Tags blocked? Use <svg>, <details>, <body>.
5. CSP BYPASS: 'self' => find JSONP/file upload. 'unsafe-eval' => eval/setTimeout/Function(). Nonce => find leak. base-uri => inject <base>.
6. WAF BYPASS: HTML entities, double encoding, mixed case, null bytes, Unicode normalization, tag mutation (mXSS).
7. DOM XSS: Trace sources (location.hash, postMessage, localStorage) to sinks (innerHTML, eval, jQuery.html). No server reflection needed.
8. STORED: Profile name rendered in admin panel or email. Test where stored values appear in different output contexts.

html.description: Reflected XSS probes for HTML body context — tests raw tag injection and script execution

probes.description: Unique marker tag injection — reflection of <swkXXXX> confirms unescaped HTML context (won't match natural tags)
img onerror XSS probe to detect event handler injection in HTML context
Script tag injection for classic reflected XSS

attribute.description: Reflected XSS probes targeting HTML attribute context — breaks out of quoted attributes to inject event handlers

probes.description: Attribute breakout via double quote with injected event handler
Attribute breakout via single quote with injected event handler
Autofocus onfocus attribute injection for no-interaction XSS

angular.description: AngularJS template injection probes — tests server-side or client-side Angular expression evaluation

probes.description: Angular expression evaluation probe — 49 confirms SSTI or client-side template injection
Angular sandbox escape using constructor chain
Alternative Angular sandbox escape via $on.constructor

javascript_context.description: XSS probes for injection inside existing <script> blocks — breaks out of string literals or script tags to execute attacker-controlled JavaScript

probes.description: Double-quote string breakout inside script block — closes string, injects alert, comments out rest of line
Single-quote string breakout with arithmetic context — subtraction operator forces alert execution as expression
Script tag breakout — closes existing script block and opens a new one with attacker-controlled code

dom_based.description: DOM XSS probes targeting client-side sinks — javascript: href injection, img onerror, and Angular constructor chain

probes.description: javascript: pseudo-protocol in href/src — triggers in DOM sinks that set href without sanitisation
img onerror DOM XSS probe using document.domain — confirms execution context
AngularJS sandbox escape via constructor chain — function in response or reflection confirms injection

stored_indicator.description: Stored XSS indicator payloads — unique markers designed to survive persistence and be detected on a different page

probes.description: Benign bold tag with unique marker — look for reflection on a DIFFERENT page to confirm stored XSS
Attribute breakout + stored onerror probe — marker reflection on another page confirms stored XSS

waf_bypass.description: WAF evasion XSS payloads from PayloadsAllTheThings — case mixing, string concat, unicode escapes, self-closing tags

probes.description: SVG onload with trailing double-slash comment — bypasses filters that require closing > bracket
HTML5 details element ontoggle — auto-fires on page load without user interaction
String concatenation WAF bypass — splits alert keyword to evade static keyword filters
Unicode escape WAF bypass — \u0061 = 'a', bypasses filters matching literal 'alert'
Mixed case script tag — bypasses case-sensitive keyword blacklists
HTML entity encoding inside SVG script — bypasses filters checking for raw parentheses
Body onload injection — effective when input is injected near the top of the page body

csp_bypass.description: CSP bypass XSS payloads -- exploit permissive CSP directives via JSONP callbacks, base-uri, trusted types, and nonce prediction

probes.description: JSONP callback XSS -- script-src 'self' bypassed via same-origin JSONP endpoint reflecting callback parameter
base-uri injection -- redirects all relative script/resource URLs to attacker domain when base-uri not restricted in CSP
CSP bypass via trusted CDN (AngularJS) -- if script-src includes cdnjs or googleapis, load Angular and use template injection
CSP override via meta tag injection -- if meta tags are injectable, overwrite CSP to allow inline scripts (only works if no HTTP header CSP)

mxss.description: Mutation XSS (mXSS) payloads -- exploit browser HTML parser normalization differences to bypass DOMPurify and server-side sanitizers

probes.description: mXSS via math/mtext context switch -- browser re-parses HTML after sanitizer, moving img out of inert context into active DOM
mXSS via SVG foreign content -- style tag in SVG is parsed differently than in HTML, img tag may escape sanitization
Nested namespace confusion mXSS -- multiple namespace switches confuse sanitizer tree-walking vs browser parsing

comment_breakout.description: HTML comment and CDATA section breakout -- escape from comment context or XML CDATA sections to inject executable content

probes.description: HTML comment breakout -- close comment with --> then inject payload, re-open comment to maintain page structure
Non-standard comment close --!> -- some browsers accept --!> as comment terminator while sanitizers may not recognize it
CDATA section breakout -- close CDATA with ]]> to inject HTML in XML/XHTML documents
